diagrams.title: State Space Search Representation
diagrams.description: Shows nodes as states (circles) and edges as actions. Initial state at left with arrow, goal state at right with double circle. Path highlighted showing solution plan.
diagrams.purpose: Visualize planning as graph traversal problem - solution is a path from initial to goal state

diagrams.title: STRIPS Action Representation
diagrams.description: Box diagram showing three components of a STRIPS action: Preconditions (top), Add List (bottom-left), Delete List (bottom-right).
diagrams.purpose: Understand how actions are represented in classical planning formalisms

diagrams.title: Forward vs Backward Search Comparison
diagrams.description: Two diagrams side by side: Left shows forward search going from initial to goal (expanding forward); Right shows backward search from goal to initial (regressing).
elements: Branching factor comparison (forward wider, backward narrower)
diagrams.purpose: Compare two main search directions in planning and their characteristics

diagrams.title: Planning Graph Structure
diagrams.description: Layered graph showing alternating state levels (S0, S1, S2) and action levels (A0, A1). Edges connect preconditions and effects between levels.
diagrams.purpose: Understand how planning graphs capture state-action progression efficiently

diagrams.title: Partial Order Plan Structure
diagrams.description: Plan as constraint graph showing actions as nodes and ordering constraints as directed edges. Some actions unordered (parallel) shown with no path between them.
diagrams.purpose: Visualize non-linear plan representation and constraint-based planning
